page: The OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided via a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the r3-256 demonstrates strong performance across multiple domains. Its single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, place it in the top 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, reaching 279.53 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 135.71 GB/sec for write operations. Database performance is notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 75.95 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the top 25%. For language model tasks, the server achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 3728.24 tokens per second on small 135M models, though performance drops to average levels on medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation combined with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers a balanced resource density for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and a complimentary public IPv4 address adds to its utility. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, software compilation, and moderate-scale text or image processing workloads.
description: The OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server instance configured with 32 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture, it delivers 256.0 GB of RAM alongside a 400 GB local NVMe SSD and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this server in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as memory bandwidth, with large-block reads reaching 279.53 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong database capabilities, processing 75.95 million Redis SET operations per second. The high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated CPU allocation make this instance well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and text processing tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud r3-256 is a memory-optimized KVM server with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe storage for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive database workloads.

256 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
400 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong memory bandwidth and database performance in benchmark testing
Suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and software compilation
